runTitle: Local dev turbopack trace - auth/session lazy import follow-up

completedImprovements.title: Defer auth/session/store heavy imports on the root path
completedImprovements.note: Moved auth-state-manager, supabase client, and browser notification service imports behind async boundaries so the initial dev startup path does not eagerly pull those modules.

pendingImprovements.title: Trace shared route compile bottleneck after auth/session lazy imports
pendingImprovements.note: Cold /api/version improved from 78s to 49s while keeping a similar 11.5MB trace, which confirms the root startup path got lighter. Total cold route responses still stayed flat at / 198s (~107.9MB trace) and /dashboard 196s (~166.0MB trace), so the main blocker remains shared route compilation after startup rather than the auth/session/store modules that were deferred.

notes: This follow-up passed npm run type-check after the lazy-import refactor.
Cold /api/version completed in 49s with trace size 11,494,640 bytes.
Cold / completed in 198s with trace size 107,940,262 bytes.
Cold /dashboard completed in 196s with trace size 166,016,313 bytes.

Compared with QA-20260422-0327, the startup probe improved materially but full route compile did not, so the next target should be route-level shared compile internals rather than more root bootstrap trimming.
